title: Writing - Part 1: Music and Entertainment
description: Your class has been discussing the role of music and entertainment in people's lives.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the roles in your notes. You should explain which role you think is most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Music has the power to change people's moods almost instantly.
Concerts and festivals create a shared sense of community.
Popular music influences fashion, language and social attitudes.

structure.introduction: Introduce music and entertainment and the two roles you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first role (e.g., providing emotional comfort) and its personal significance.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second role (e.g., bringing people together) and its social importance.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both roles and state which you think is most important.

content.modelAnswer:
Music and entertainment have been central to human culture throughout history. From ancient rituals to modern streaming platforms, they serve numerous important functions in our lives and societies. This essay will consider how music provides emotional comfort and how it helps bring people together.

One of music's most powerful qualities is its ability to influence our emotions. When people feel sad, anxious or overwhelmed, listening to music can provide immediate comfort and relief. Many people describe music as their main way of managing stress, and research confirms that it can reduce anxiety and even help with conditions such as depression. For this reason, music plays an important therapeutic role in many people's daily lives.

Music also has a remarkable ability to unite people. Concerts, festivals and shared playlists create bonds between individuals who might otherwise have little in common. Music transcends language, age and cultural barriers, bringing strangers together in moments of shared joy. At times of national or international crisis, songs have united entire populations and become symbols of hope.

In conclusion, while both roles are significant, I believe music's power to unite people is its most important quality. Emotional comfort, though vital, is largely personal. The social bonds that music creates, however, have the potential to strengthen entire communities and even promote peace between nations.
